what_it_does: Implements a coverage-complete code-archaeology dragnet that walks the real repository tree, parses files for deterministic structure, runs a two-stage LLM prose cascade, embeds the resulting records into a registered code_archaeology corpus space, and writes a fail-loud ledger proving every file is accounted for.
observations: Uses os.walk over the real filesystem tree instead of git-ls-files so git-ignored content directories are not silently dropped.
Excludes junk directories and sensitive files by name, extension, or substring hint before parsing or embedding.
Separates deterministic structural parsing from LLM prose: Python AST, regex for JS/TS imports/exports, and JSON keys.
Runs a coarse description for every readable file and a fine description only for code-bearing files with symbols.
Reuses cog.run_items for LLM batches and Suite.capture_corpus for corpus capture.
Builds a deterministic field index M2 for registry/type discoverability without any model calls.
Provides an ingest_batch seam for integration with cc_dragnet.
Writes a ledger that records per-file state and enforces denominator equals the sum of all states.
Captures records in 200-item batches and drops the fingerprint and scratch text from the embeddable payload.
standouts: Coverage-correctness via real-tree enumeration and loud per-exclusion reasons.
Parser-first separation of deterministic structure from LLM-generated prose.
Fail-loud ledger invariant that raises if any file is unaccounted.
conventions: Heavy use of module-level constants for classification sets.
Pydantic schemas for typed LLM outputs.
code:// URI addressing for records and ledger entries.
Sibling-store JSONL pattern for the field index.
Batch capture and timestamped ledger rows.
concerns: JS/TS parsing relies on regex and will miss dynamic imports, re-exports, and TypeScript-specific constructs.
Sensitive-file detection uses substring matching on the basename, which can over-exclude or under-exclude files.
The coverage percentage subtracts all excluded files from the denominator, so broad exclusions can hide gaps.
Generic parser silently returns empty symbols when JSON decoding fails.
ingest_batch imports runtime modules inside function bodies, making dependencies implicit.
Batch size and model endpoint assumptions are hardcoded rather than exposed as CLI options.
notes: A build-prep primitive that tries to guarantee no repository file is silently ignored before synthesis.
questions: What is the exact contract of cog.run_items and Suite.capture_corpus expected by this file?
How are files whose language is not in _LANG_BY_EXT handled?
What happens when a file is classified as code-bearing but parse_file returns zero symbols?
Is the field index intended to be append-only or overwritten on each build?
How are incremental updates handled when only file fingerprints change?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a coverage-complete, queryable map of a repo produced before significant builds; the file implements that pipeline end-to-end, including enumeration, parsing, LLM prose, embedding, ledger, and a deterministic field index, so purpose and actual align.
apparent_themes: Security-first exclusion of secrets and credentials
intention_signals: The file is trying to eliminate the recurring failure mode where agents sample a repo partially, grow confident, and build from incomplete information, by making exhaustive, explainable coverage a prerequisite.
novelty: The explicit real-tree denominator, per-exclusion reasoning, and the fail-loud ledger invariant are unusual for a repo-mapping script; the parser-first/LLM-prose-only split is also a deliberate architectural choice.
contribution: It contributes a reusable, auditable build-prep primitive that turns an entire repository into a queryable corpus with deterministic structural facts and neutral prose summaries, while recording why anything was left out.
summary_for_embedding: ops/code_archaeology.py is a build-prep dragnet that enumerates the real repository tree, parses files for deterministic symbols and imports, runs a coarse-to-fine LLM prose cascade, embeds the results into a code_archaeology corpus, and writes a fail-loud coverage ledger.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to ensure every repository file is accounted for and described before synthesis, preventing builds from relying on partial or sampled information.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC timestamp as an ISO-8601 string.
symbols.description: A small utility used throughout the module to timestamp ledger rows and summary records consistently.

symbols.does: Decides whether a directory is vendored, generated, cache, or a virtualenv.
symbols.description: Checks hardcoded junk names, site-packages, venv name suffixes, and the presence of pyvenv.cfg to avoid enumerating non-source directories.

symbols.does: Walks the real filesystem tree and returns included files plus a list of excluded directories with reasons.
symbols.description: Uses os.walk, prunes junk directories, records each pruning with a reason, and optionally force-includes git-ignored content directories.

symbols.does: Classifies a file into a coarse kind such as test, doc, config, module, script, asset, or other.
symbols.description: Combines path heuristics, language, and whether parsed symbols exist to decide if a file should advance to fine-grained LLM analysis.

symbols.does: Extracts top-level symbols, imports, and registry sentinels from Python source using the ast module.
symbols.description: Walks the AST for function and class definitions, import statements, and module-level assignments to registry sentinel names like ROLE or PROJECTION.

symbols.does: Extracts best-effort symbols and imports for JavaScript, TypeScript, and JSON files.
symbols.description: Uses regular expressions to find import/require statements and export declarations in JS/TS, and top-level dictionary keys in JSON.

symbols.does: Reads a single file, applies security and binary exclusions, and returns a structural skeleton.
symbols.description: Checks sensitive-name and binary-content rules, decodes UTF-8, computes a SHA-256 fingerprint, counts lines, dispatches to language-specific parsers, and classifies the file kind.

symbols.does: Pydantic schema for the coarse LLM prose output.
symbols.description: Requires only a what_it_is string, keeping the first-stage description cheap and uniform across all files.

symbols.does: Pydantic schema for the fine LLM prose output.
symbols.description: Requires a neutral summary, a list of registry touchpoints, and a list of cross-references for code-bearing files.

symbols.does: Constructs the Role object used for coarse descriptions.
symbols.description: Returns a runtime Role configured with a neutral prompt template and the CoarseOut schema.

symbols.does: Constructs the Role object used for fine descriptions.
symbols.description: Returns a runtime Role configured with a neutral prompt template and the FineOut schema.

symbols.does: Formats a skeleton into the utterance passed to the coarse role.
symbols.description: Includes path, language, kind, line count, top symbols, and a head excerpt so the LLM can state what the file is.

symbols.does: Formats a skeleton into the utterance passed to the fine role.
symbols.description: Includes path, language, parsed symbols, imports, declared registry types, and a head excerpt for richer summarization.

symbols.does: Runs the coarse LLM over all skeletons, then runs the fine LLM over code-bearing gated files.
symbols.description: Uses cog.run_items for both stages, assembles records with parser fields plus LLM prose, builds embeddable text, and returns records and throughput statistics.

symbols.does: Builds a code:// URI for a project-relative path.
symbols.description: A small addressing helper used to name corpus records and ledger entries consistently.

symbols.does: Enumerates and parses the repository deterministically to emit typed field rows.
symbols.description: Produces a sibling JSONL index mapping code addresses to fields like kind, language, symbol, declares, and imports, without invoking any model.

symbols.does: Scans the field index JSONL for targets matching a field/value pair.
symbols.description: Supports exact or substring matching by reading the index linearly at query time.

symbols.does: Provides the cc_dragnet ingest-batch seam over an input directory and channel.
symbols.description: Wraps enumeration, parsing, the LLM cascade, and corpus capture for an external caller, returning addresses, counts, exclusions, and errors.

symbols.does: CLI entry point that dispatches sample runs, full M1 runs, field-index builds, and field queries.
symbols.description: Parses arguments, validates the registered code_archaeology space, runs the parser and cascade, captures records in batches, writes the ledger, and enforces the fail-loud coverage invariant.
